bank_purpose: Move students from accurate decoding into quick meaning proof so decoding and language stay linked.
teacher_note_on_meaning: Students should decode accurately, then name the meaning clue that proves they understood the word.

starter_examples.explanation: mis- signals wrong; misread means read wrongly.

core_examples.explanation: port signals carry; transport connects across + carry.
-less signals without; lifeless means without life.

stretch_examples.explanation: geo signals earth; geology is the study of earth.

fake.explanation: mallook is not a standard real word.

near_miss.explanation: Students may stop at transport and miss the ending signal.
near_miss.teacher_note: Good for proving full-word reading, not just chunk spotting.

arguable.explanation: One option is the cleaner morphology read; one is the more common everyday interpretation.
arguable.teacher_note: Use only if you want to compare formal meaning with student language.

teacher_note_on_traps: Trap rounds should expose partial decoding or loose meaning proof, not punish effort.
sentence_target_bank: The crate is transportable because two people can carry it.
He misread the schedule and missed the bus.
repair_target_bank: The word geology means the study of life.
starter_notes: Keep the prove-it options concrete and short.
core_notes: Require students to name the meaning signal after accurate reading.
stretch_notes: Use arguable meanings only if you will resolve the stronger interpretation clearly.
usage_notes: This bank works best when decoding and meaning happen in the same turn.
